mnemonics: CONFIDENCE: Captures Our Notional, Fixed Interval Demonstrating Estimated, Normal Certainty Extents
keyPoints: A confidence interval provides a range of plausible values for an unknown population parameter, constructed from sample data.
The confidence level (e.g., 95%) indicates the long-run frequency that the method produces an interval containing the true parameter if repeated many times.
The width of the interval is influenced by sample size (n), variability (σ), and the chosen confidence level.
commonMistakes: Interpreting a 95% confidence interval as there being a 95% probability the specific interval contains the true parameter (the parameter is fixed, the interval is random).
Assuming the confidence interval implies 95% of the sample data points lie within the interval (it pertains to the parameter estimate, not the data distribution).
